channel.title: briefUpdatesHealthOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesHealthOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: This complex brain network may explain many of Parkinson's stranger symptoms
item.description: Parkinson's disease appears to disrupt a brain network involved in everything from movement to memory.

item.title: Hospitals are posting prices for patients. It's mostly industry using the data
item.description: The Trump administration pushed for price transparency in health care. But instead of patients shopping for services, it's mostly health systems and i...

item.title: In a world built for sitting, here's how to stay active — even when stuck inside
item.description: In the office, classroom and living room, working and relaxing mean sitting still. Our bodies evolved without chairs. Here are some tips for getting o...

item.title: US Cancer Institute Studying Ivermectin’s ‘Ability To Kill Cancer Cells’
item.description: At a January event organized by allies of health secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr., National Cancer Institute Director Anthony Letai said results may be...

item.title: Obamacare Sign-Ups Drop, but the Extent Won’t Be Clear for Months
item.description: Experts say Affordable Care Act sign-up data won’t be clear until people who were enrolled have paid — or haven't paid — their new, often much higher,...

item.title: La consulta con tu próximo médico de atención primaria podría ser solo virtual y agendada a través de IA
item.description: La falta de médicos de atención primaria es un problema nacional. Algunas grandes redes de salud están recurriendo a la inteligencia artificial en bus...

item.title: This Ballad Hospital, Flooded by Hurricane Helene, Will Be Rebuilt for $44M in a Flood Plain
item.description: Ballad Health, the nation’s largest state-sanctioned hospital monopoly, plans to rebuild Unicoi County Hospital on land that two climate modeling comp...

item.title: Olympic COVID restrictions are gone, but some athletes are still self-quarantining
item.description: For most people, the pandemic days of masking are behind them. In certain corners of the Winter Olympics, though, things still look a lot like they di...

item.title: Modest 'mental exercise' can reduce risk of dementia for decades, study finds
item.description: One type of cognitive training appears to reduce the risk of dementia 20 years later.

item.title: Carta Healthcare – AI for Registry Creation
item.description: It’s not well known but there’s a lot of people in hospitals who spend a lot of time creating patient registries for quality programs, CMS reporting, ...

item.title: Watch: Is MAHA the New MAGA?
item.description: With fractures emerging in the Make America Great Again movement, some Republicans are looking to capitalize on its “MAHA” counterpart ahead of the mi...

item.title: Will HHS Enhance or Stall the Promise of Artificial Intelligence for Healthcare?
item.description: By STEVEN ZECOLA In its Strategy for Artificial Intelligence (V.3), the Department of Health and Human Services (“HHS”) acknowledges that: “For too lo...

item.title: Arnold Ventures Part II “Structuring Information Felicitously”
item.description: By JEFF GOLDSMITH In the first part of our look at Arnold Ventures, we explored its business model and generous support of elite University health pol...

item.title: Primary care, Concierge Care & AI – How to fix health care

item.title: John Arnold: The Most Powerful Man in Healthcare Nobody has Ever Heard Of (Pt I)
item.description: By JEFF GOLDSMITH It has happened at least a dozen times. I mention John Arnold and am greeted by knowledgeable healthcare colleagues with a blank sta...
